prompt_text: Open source or affordable knowledge management tools instead of Confluence
response:
# Knowledge Management Alternatives to Confluence

## Open Source Options

### Documentation-Focused
- **Docusaurus** - React-based, excellent for technical docs, GitHub-hosted
- **MkDocs** - Python-based, lightweight, Markdown-driven
- **Gitbook** - Free tier available, Git-synced content
- **Outline** - Modern wiki, self-hostable, clean UI
- **MediaWiki** - Battle-tested (powers Wikipedia), self-hosted

### Wiki/General Purpose
- **TiddlyWiki** - Single HTML file wiki, very portable
- **Zim** - Desktop wiki, works offline
- **Joplin** - Note-taking, end-to-end encrypted, open source
- **Obsidian** (freemium) - Local-first, powerful linking

## Affordable/Freemium

| Tool | Price | Best For |
|------|-------|----------|
| **Notion** | Free → $8-15/user | All-purpose, relational |
| **Coda** | Free → custom | Docs + light database |
| **Slite** | ~$5/user | Team knowledge, clean UX |
| **Nuclino** | Free → $10/user | Lightweight wiki |
| **Bookstack** | Free (self-hosted) | Simple wiki feel |

## Budget-Friendly Hybrid

- **GitHub Wiki + GitHub Pages** - Free, developer-friendly
- **Notion + free tier** - Works for small teams
- **Bookstack self-hosted** - One-time server cost

**What matters most to you?** (Search, collaboration, offline access, migration ease?) That'll help narrow it down.
